<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Onoue Kurumayama Kofun]][[en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]][[ja:尾上車山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Onoue Kurumayama Kofun]][[en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]][[ja:尾上車山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Onoue Kurumayama Kofun]][[en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]][[ja:尾上車山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28685132]] Wikidata name: Onoue Kurumayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Onoe Kurumayama Kofun | native_name =尾上車山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Onoue Kurumayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Onoe Kurumayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Onoe Kurumayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.658966666667|N|133.86514166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Okayama|WD=Q200078}} | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|32.28|N|133|51|54.51|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Onoe Kurumayama Kofun'''|尾上車山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Onoe neighborhood of [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/206263|title=尾上車山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|"Girigiriyama Kofun"|ぎりぎり山古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Onoe Kurumayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located at the southeastern end of Kibi no Nakayama, an independent hill to the west of Okayama city.The tumulus is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138.5 meters, and a round posterior portion with a diameter of about 96 meters and a height of about 11 meters and was built in three stages. The anterior part has a width of 52 meters, height of nine meters and was built in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and shards of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found, and it is presumed that there is a 20-meter long extension on the north side of the mound. The tumulus has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but has a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis of the mound in the center of the round posterior portion. Based on its shape and the ''haniwa'' uncovered, the tumulus was constructed in the middle or latter half of the 4th century, in the first half of the early Kofun period. As it was situated on the shore of the [[Seto Inland Sea]] when completed, it is speculated that it was intended to be seen by maritime traffic as an indication of the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 138.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high x 2 steps ;Constriction width: 38 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 96 meter diameter x 11 meters high x 3 steps
<gallery> File:Onoue Kurumayama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Rear circular mound top File:Onoue Kurumayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Looking forward from the rear circular part </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Onoue Kurumayama Kofun]][[en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]][[ja:尾上車山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Onoue Kurumayama Kofun]][[en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]][[ja:尾上車山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Onoue Kurumayama Kofun]][[en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]][[ja:尾上車山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28685132]] Wikidata name: Onoue Kurumayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Onoe Kurumayama Kofun | native_name =尾上車山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Onoue Kurumayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Onoe Kurumayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Onoe Kurumayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.658966666667|N|133.86514166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Okayama|WD=Q200078}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|32.28|N|133|51|54.51|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Onoe Kurumayama Kofun'''|尾上車山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Onoe neighborhood of {{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|en|Kita-ku, Okayama|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/206263|title=尾上車山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|"Girigiriyama Kofun"|ぎりぎり山古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Onoe Kurumayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located at the southeastern end of Kibi no Nakayama, an independent hill to the west of Okayama city.The tumulus is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138.5 meters, and a round posterior portion with a diameter of about 96 meters and a height of about 11 meters and was built in three stages. The anterior part has a width of 52 meters, height of nine meters and was built in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and shards of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found, and it is presumed that there is a 20-meter long extension on the north side of the mound. The tumulus has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but has a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis of the mound in the center of the round posterior portion. Based on its shape and the ''haniwa'' uncovered, the tumulus was constructed in the middle or latter half of the 4th century, in the first half of the early Kofun period. As it was situated on the shore of the [[Seto Inland Sea]] when completed, it is speculated that it was intended to be seen by maritime traffic as an indication of the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 138.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high x 2 steps ;Constriction width: 38 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 96 meter diameter x 11 meters high x 3 steps
<gallery> File:Onoue Kurumayama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Rear circular mound top File:Onoue Kurumayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Looking forward from the rear circular part </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

As it was situated on the shore of the [[Seto Inland Sea]] when completed, it is speculated that it was intended to be seen by maritime traffic as an indication of the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Onoe Kurumayama Kofun | native_name =尾上車山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Onoue Kurumayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Onoe Kurumayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Onoe Kurumayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.658966666667|N|133.86514166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Okayama|WD=Q200078}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|32.28|N|133|51|54.51|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Onoe Kurumayama Kofun'''|尾上車山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Onoe neighborhood of {{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|en|Kita-ku, Okayama|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/206263|title=尾上車山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|"Girigiriyama Kofun"|ぎりぎり山古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Onoe Kurumayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located at the southeastern end of Kibi no Nakayama, an independent hill to the west of Okayama city.The tumulus is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138.5 meters, and a round posterior portion with a diameter of about 96 meters and a height of about 11 meters and was built in three stages. The anterior part has a width of 52 meters, height of nine meters and was built in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and shards of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found, and it is presumed that there is a 20-meter long extension on the north side of the mound. The tumulus has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but has a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis of the mound in the center of the round posterior portion. Based on its shape and the ''haniwa'' uncovered, the tumulus was constructed in the middle or latter half of the 4th century, in the first half of the early Kofun period. As it was situated on the shore of the [[Seto Inland Sea]] when completed, it is speculated that it was intended to be seen by maritime traffic as an indication of the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It is located at the southeastern end of Kibi no Nakayama, an independent hill to the west of Okayama city.The tumulus is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138.5 meters, and a round posterior portion with a diameter of about 96 meters and a height of about 11 meters and was built in three stages. The anterior part has a width of 52 meters, height of nine meters and was built in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and shards of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found, and it is presumed that there is a 20-meter long extension on the north side of the mound. The tumulus has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but has a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis of the mound in the center of the round posterior portion. Based on its shape and the ''haniwa'' uncovered, the tumulus was constructed in the middle or latter half of the 4th century, in the first half of the early Kofun period. As it was situated on the shore of the [[Seto Inland Sea]] when completed, it is speculated that it was intended to be seen by maritime traffic as an indication of the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 138.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high x 2 steps ;Constriction width: 38 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 96 meter diameter x 11 meters high x 3 steps

{{nihongo|'''Onoe Kurumayama Kofun'''|尾上車山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Onoe neighborhood of {{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|en|Kita-ku, Okayama|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku, Okayama|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/206263|title=尾上車山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|"Girigiriyama Kofun"|ぎりぎり山古墳||}}.
==Overview== The Onoe Kurumayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located at the southeastern end of Kibi no Nakayama, an independent hill to the west of Okayama city.The tumulus is orientated to the east, has a total length of about 138.5 meters, and a round posterior portion with a diameter of about 96 meters and a height of about 11 meters and was built in three stages. The anterior part has a width of 52 meters, height of nine meters and was built in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and shards of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found, and it is presumed that there is a 20-meter long extension on the north side of the mound. The tumulus has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but has a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis of the mound in the center of the round posterior portion. Based on its shape and the ''haniwa'' uncovered, the tumulus was constructed in the middle or latter half of the 4th century, in the first half of the early Kofun period. As it was situated on the shore of the [[Seto Inland Sea]] when completed, it is speculated that it was intended to be seen by maritime traffic as an indication of the power of the ancient [[Kingdom of Kibi]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 138.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high x 2 steps ;Constriction width: 38 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 96 meter diameter x 11 meters high x 3 steps
<gallery> File:Onoue Kurumayama Kofun, funchou.jpg|Rear circular mound top File:Onoue Kurumayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Looking forward from the rear circular part </gallery>
